team_context.cap_situation: healthy — ~$50M in cap space after releasing Stefon Diggs and making initial free agency signings. Already invested heavily in 2025 FA class ($209M guaranteed) and continuing to add in 2026.
team_context.trade_posture: Aggressive buyers. Came off 14-3 season and Super Bowl LX appearance. Mike Vrabel's second year — team is firmly in win-now mode while Drake Maye is on a rookie deal. Will add talent at positions of need (WR, EDGE, OL depth) but unlikely to trade core pieces. 11 draft picks including 5 in first 4 rounds give them capital to trade for upgrades.

players.name: Drake Maye
players.performance_evidence: 4,394 yards, 31 TDs, 8 INTs, 72% completion rate (led NFL) in 2025. Second-team All-Pro and Pro Bowl selection. Led Patriots from 4-13 to 14-3 and Super Bowl appearance. MVP-caliber second season on a rookie deal.

trade_value.reasoning: Franchise quarterback on a rookie deal with 2 years remaining plus a 5th-year option. Elite production at 23 years old. This is the single most valuable asset type in the NFL — a young franchise QB on cost-controlled money. No realistic trade value can capture this; he's worth multiple 1st-round picks.

players.availability_reasoning: Face of the franchise. Led team to Super Bowl in year 2. On a massively team-friendly rookie deal. Mike Vrabel cited Maye as a reason for taking the job. Extension discussions will begin in 2027. Zero chance of a trade under any circumstances.

players.name: Joshua Dobbs
players.performance_evidence: Veteran journeyman backup. Did not see meaningful action in 2025. Career 11 starts across multiple teams. Valued for locker room presence and ability to spot-start if needed.

trade_value.reasoning: Experienced backup QB on a cheap 1-year deal. Minimal trade value but teams always need emergency QB depth.

players.availability_reasoning: Standard veteran backup. Team would move him if they found a better option or if another team was desperate for a QB, but there's no urgency to trade him.

players.name: Rhamondre Stevenson
players.performance_evidence: 603 rushing yards, 345 receiving yards, 9 total TDs in 2025 while sharing backfield with Henderson. Excellent pass protector — Drake Maye praised his blocking. Was briefly demoted early in season for fumbling but rebounded strongly. 1,040-yard season in 2022.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid RB1 but turning 28 with Henderson emerging as the future. Contract ($9M AAV) is market-rate for RBs, and the position depreciates quickly. Worth a 4th-round pick given age curve and Henderson's presence.

players.availability_reasoning: Team drafted TreVeyon Henderson in the 2nd round in 2025 specifically to eventually take over. Henderson led the team in rushing yards in 2025. Stevenson's cap hit escalates to $9.6M in 2027 and $11.6M in 2028. Could be a trade candidate if a contender needs a reliable RB, especially given the team could get out cleanly after 2026.
players.injury_notes: Toe injury caused him to miss 3 games in 2025. Fumbling issues earlier in career.

players.name: TreVeyon Henderson
players.performance_evidence: Led Patriots in rushing yards as a rookie despite splitting carries with Stevenson. Had explosive two-TD game at Tampa Bay with 55-yard and 69-yard TD runs. Named NFL Offensive Rookie of the Month for November. Dynamic speed back with 4.33 speed.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, explosive RB on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. His speed and big-play ability on a cost-controlled contract make him worth a late 2nd/early 3rd, though RB positional value limits the ceiling.

players.availability_reasoning: Henderson is the future of the backfield and the team invested a 2nd-round pick in him. They wouldn't move him unless blown away by an offer. His rookie deal and upside make him a core piece going forward.
players.injury_notes: Suffered a concussion in Week 16 vs. Baltimore but cleared protocol.

players.name: Romeo Doubs
players.performance_evidence: Career-high 724 receiving yards and 55 catches with 6 TDs in 2025 with Packers. Career totals: 202 catches, 2,424 yards, 21 TDs in 59 games. Strong playoff performer (20 catches, 371 yards in 4 postseason games). Has never topped 1,000 yards in a season.

trade_value.reasoning: Just signed a 4-year, $80M deal making him the WR1 for Drake Maye. Young (26 in April) with 4 years of control. The contract is a slight overpay relative to production, but the positional need and length of deal provide value. Worth a mid-2nd to a WR-needy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a massive free agent deal literally yesterday to replace Stefon Diggs. The Patriots invested $80M specifically to give Drake Maye a weapon. Trading him would be franchise malpractice. He's untouchable for at least the 2026 season.
players.character_notes: Was suspended by Packers for 1 game in 2024 for missing practices and meetings. Patriots reportedly vetted the situation thoroughly before signing.

players.name: Kayshon Boutte
players.performance_evidence: Emerged as a legitimate weapon in 2025 — led team with 5 receptions for 93 yards and 2 TDs in multiple games. Had a spectacular 32-yard one-handed TD catch in the divisional playoff round vs. Houston. Still developing but showed big-play ability and chemistry with Maye. 6th-round pick who beat the odds.

trade_value.reasoning: Young WR who showed flashes in 2025 but on an expiring deal. The contract situation (final year, cheap) is attractive for a team looking for a low-cost flier, but the limited body of work caps the return at a 4th/5th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Boutte has developed real chemistry with Maye and showed improvement. With Doubs now in the fold as WR1, Boutte's role shifts but he's still valued. Team would want to extend him or let him prove it in 2026 rather than trade him cheaply.
players.injury_notes: Ruled out for a game with a concussion in late 2025.

players.name: Mack Hollins
players.performance_evidence: Veteran deep threat and special teams contributor. Signed in 2025 free agency. Provides depth and leadership in the WR room. Not a primary weapon but fills a role as a WR3/WR4.

trade_value.reasoning: 32-year-old WR3 on an expiring deal. Minimal standalone trade value — essentially a roster body. Maybe a conditional 7th-round pick to a team needing WR depth at the deadline.

players.availability_reasoning: Veteran role player on a 1-year deal. If a contender comes calling at midseason needing WR depth, the Patriots would move him for a late pick, especially with Doubs now in the room.
players.injury_notes: Ruled out with an abdomen injury late in 2025.

players.name: Hunter Henry
players.performance_evidence: Reliable veteran TE. Two-TD game vs. Pittsburgh in 2025. Has been the team's top pass-catching TE since 2021. Career totals over 400+ catches. Trusted target for Maye, especially in the red zone and on intermediate routes.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid TE1 but in the final year of his deal at age 31 with an $11.75M cap hit. The expiring contract and age limit value significantly. Worth a 4th-round pick to a contender needing a TE.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of contract, turning 31. Austin Hooper departed but team signed Julian Hill. If the Patriots draft or sign a TE, Henry becomes available, especially given the cap hit. His reliability makes him a target for contenders at the trade deadline.

players.name: CJ Dippre
players.performance_evidence: Rookie 5th-round pick in 2025. Used primarily as a blocker and special teams contributor. Limited receiving production but valued for his blocking ability in the Patriots' run game.

trade_value.reasoning: Developmental TE2 on a cheap rookie deal. Essentially no trade value as a standalone piece — maybe a conditional 7th.

players.availability_reasoning: Low-cost depth piece. Team wouldn't block a trade but also nobody's calling for him. He has developmental upside but hasn't shown enough to generate trade interest.

players.name: Will Campbell
players.performance_evidence: Started 13 games as a rookie. PFF overall grade of 72.6 (32nd among tackles), pass-blocking grade of 76.1 (23rd) — best rookie pass protector in 2025 per PFF. However, struggled badly in the postseason, allowing a league-worst 14 pressures in the Super Bowl. Has upside but needs development, especially in run blocking (67.7 grade).

trade_value.reasoning: 4th overall pick on a rookie deal protecting a franchise QB. Despite the Super Bowl meltdown, his regular season pass-blocking was solid for a rookie and he's only 22. OT is a premium position. The investment and upside make him worth a late 1st-round pick, though his trade value was dented by the postseason collapse.

players.availability_reasoning: 4th overall pick taken specifically to protect Drake Maye. Despite the Super Bowl struggles, the team publicly committed to him. There's debate about moving him to guard but the Patriots won't trade him — they invested too much draft capital and he showed enough in the regular season. He's the long-term LT.
players.injury_notes: MCL injury caused him to miss 4 games late in the regular season (placed on IR Nov 26, activated Jan 3). Returned for playoffs but struggled.
players.trade_buzz: Some media speculation about a position change to guard, but no trade buzz.

players.name: Alijah Vera-Tucker
players.performance_evidence: Former 1st-round pick. When healthy, excellent guard — PFF 77.7 grade in 2024, 4th-lowest pressure rate allowed (2.8%). However, has played only 43 of a possible 85 career games due to torn triceps (2022), torn Achilles (2023), and torn triceps again (missed all of 2025). Just signed with Patriots in March 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: High-ceiling guard on a new 3-year deal, but the catastrophic injury history makes this a massive gamble. When healthy, he's a Pro Bowl-caliber player. The contract has significant game-day roster bonuses to mitigate risk. Worth a mid-to-late 3rd-round pick if healthy, but the injury profile suppresses value.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed this week in free agency. Team expects him to start at LG between Campbell and Wilson. Not being shopped, but the injury-laden contract is structured for possible early exit. Would need a solid offer to move him given the O-line is a clear area of need.
players.injury_notes: CRITICAL: Has suffered 3 season-ending injuries in 5 NFL seasons — torn triceps (2022), torn Achilles (2023), torn triceps on other arm (missed all 2025). Only played 43 of 85 possible games. Availability is the single biggest question.

players.name: Jared Wilson
players.performance_evidence: Rookie 3rd-round pick who started at LG in 2025. Moving to center in 2026 after the Patriots traded Garrett Bradbury to the Bears. Struggled along with Campbell in the postseason but showed enough upside as a rookie to keep the starting job. Still developing.

trade_value.reasoning: Young center on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. Limited track record (rookie moving to a new position) but cost-controlled interior linemen have value. Worth a mid-to-late 4th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Expected to be the starting center in 2026 after Bradbury trade. The team needs him — they wouldn't move him without a clear upgrade plan. Only available if team drafts or signs a better center option.
players.injury_notes: Ruled out with a concussion late in the 2025 season.

players.name: Mike Onwenu
players.performance_evidence: One of the best guards in football. Versatile — has played both guard spots and right tackle at a high level. Originally a 6th-round pick who earned a top-market contract ($57M/3yr). Anchor of the Patriots offensive line through the Super Bowl run.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl-caliber guard but in the final year of his deal with a massive $25M cap hit. The expiring contract limits trade value. Worth a high 2nd-round pick to a contender willing to eat the cap hit, but the dead cap ($7.5M) vs. cap savings ($17.5M) makes a trade financially possible.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of contract at a $25M cap hit — highest on the team's O-line. Spotrac flagged him as an extension candidate. If the Patriots can't extend him and he's headed to free agency, he becomes a trade candidate at midseason. But he's the best lineman on the team and they're competing, so the price would be steep.
players.trade_buzz: Spotrac identified Onwenu as an extension candidate this offseason.

players.name: Morgan Moses
players.performance_evidence: Durable veteran RT — started 16 games in 2025 at age 33. Has started 140+ consecutive regular season games across his career. Reliable pass protector who doesn't miss time. Limited upside but steady.

trade_value.reasoning: 34-year-old RT on a reasonable deal. Age severely limits trade value despite his durability and consistency. Worth a 5th-round pick at best — teams can find cheaper RT options.

players.availability_reasoning: Aging veteran on a team that might look to get younger at RT. If a rookie or FA emerges, Moses becomes expendable. The $5M dead cap is manageable. Could be moved at the deadline to a contender needing OL help.
players.injury_notes: Brief injury absences in 2025 (left games at Cincinnati and Baltimore) but always returned quickly. Remarkable durability for his age.

players.name: Christian Barmore
players.performance_evidence: Started 16 games in 2025 after recovering from blood clots that affected his 2024 season. Formed a strong DT tandem with Milton Williams. 4-year, $83M extension signed in 2024. However, ESPN has noted his production has fallen off and he may need a 'change of scenery.'

trade_value.reasoning: Talented interior defender on a team-friendly deal (potential out after 2026 with only $4.3M in cap savings). The dead cap ($12.8M) makes a pre-June 1 trade expensive, but the talent and age make him worth a late 2nd/early 3rd to a team with cap space. Production concerns and off-field issues limit the ceiling.

players.availability_reasoning: ESPN reported Barmore may need a 'change of scenery' and could be playing elsewhere in 2026. The Patriots have Milton Williams as their top DT. The dead cap issue ($12.8M) makes a trade costly but the $4.3M in cap savings and freeing up the roster spot could make it worthwhile, especially as a post-June 1 designation.
players.injury_notes: Dealt with blood clots in 2024 that threatened his career. Recovered and played a full season in 2025 but the health concern looms.
players.character_notes: ESPN cited off-field issues contributing to potential departure.
players.trade_buzz: ESPN specifically named Barmore as a 'shocking candidate to leave the Patriots this offseason.' Beat reporters have noted he may be on thin ice.

players.name: Eric Gregory
players.performance_evidence: Rookie 5th-round pick from Missouri. Rotational DT who contributed as a run-stuffer and provided interior depth behind Barmore and Williams. Listed on Spotrac at $1.005M cap hit for 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Depth DT on a minimum rookie deal. Essentially no standalone trade value — conditional 7th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Rotational depth piece. No one is calling about him, but the team wouldn't block a deal if someone offered a late pick.

players.name: Milton Williams
players.performance_evidence: Highest-paid player in Patriots history ($104M/4yr). Exceeded expectations in 2025 — played 60% of defensive snaps despite missing 5 games to injury. Key sack of Justin Herbert to clinch wild card win. Patriots allowed 3.7 yards/rush with him on field vs. 5.0 without. Super Bowl champion with Eagles in 2024.

trade_value.reasoning: 27-year-old ascending interior defender with 3 years remaining on a mega-deal. The massive guaranteed money ($40M+ remaining) and dead cap ($42M) makes a trade essentially impossible. If he were tradeable, he'd be worth a 1st-round pick based on talent and age. But the financials lock him in.

players.availability_reasoning: Highest-paid player in franchise history with $42M in dead cap if traded. The financials make it mathematically impossible to trade him. He's the centerpiece of the defensive line and the team's biggest investment.
players.injury_notes: Missed 5 games in 2025 due to injury (ankle). Questions about whether he could handle an increased workload coming from Philly's rotation were answered positively.

players.name: Harold Landry III
players.performance_evidence: 2021 Pro Bowler (12 sacks). Had 10.5 sacks in 2023, 9 sacks in 2024 with Tennessee. First year in New England in 2025 — brought leadership and Vrabel system knowledge. PFF run defense grade of 82.2 in 2024 (6th among edge rushers). Sacked QBs in multiple key moments.

trade_value.reasoning: Productive EDGE entering his age-30 season with 2 years and $16.5M cap hit. Not elite anymore but a consistent 8-10 sack producer. Worth a 3rd-round pick to a contender needing pass rush. Cap savings of $8.5M if traded makes it financially feasible.

players.availability_reasoning: Key veteran on a competing team, but turning 30 with a significant cap hit. If the Patriots sign or draft a younger EDGE (they've been linked to Maxx Crosby and added Dre'Mont Jones), Landry could become expendable, especially in 2027. For now, he's a starter who'd only move for significant compensation.
players.injury_notes: Torn ACL in August 2022, missed entire 2022 season. Bounced back well. Ruled out with knee injury late in 2025.

players.name: Robert Spillane
players.performance_evidence: 97 total tackles, 1 sack, 2 INTs, 5 passes defended in 2025 (13 games). Career-high 158 tackles with Raiders in 2024 with an 87.3 run-defense grade (8th in NFL per PFF). UDFA success story who became a quality NFL starter. Played 98.2% of defensive snaps with Raiders in 2024.

trade_value.reasoning: Reliable off-ball LB turning 30 with 2 years left at $11M AAV. Solid but not elite. Worth a 3rd/4th-round pick. Age and position (LB is replaceable) limit value, but the guaranteed money and dead cap make a trade complicated.

players.availability_reasoning: Core defensive player and locker room leader. His guaranteed salary through 2026 and dead cap make him a de facto lock. Would only be moved if the Patriots found a younger, cheaper replacement in the draft.
players.injury_notes: Ruled out with ankle injury late in 2025 but played 13 games overall.

players.name: Christian Elliss
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2024 season with 80 tackles and 1.5 sacks. Carried an expanded role in 2025 alongside Spillane as a starting linebacker. 7th-round pick who developed into a solid contributor through hard work and special teams value.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality depth LB on a cheap expiring deal. Minimal trade value — worth a 5th/6th-round pick at best. Useful but replaceable.

players.availability_reasoning: On a cheap deal with an expiring contract. The team values his contribution but wouldn't block a trade if a pick were offered. With Spillane and a new signing (K.J. Britt) in the LB room, Elliss has competition.

players.name: Dre'Mont Jones
players.performance_evidence: Career-high 7 sacks and 55 pressures in 2025 split between Titans and Ravens. 11.4% pressure rate led Baltimore. Versatile — can play DT and DE. 7 NFL seasons, 22+ sacks with Broncos, plus productive stints in Seattle, Tennessee, and Baltimore.

trade_value.reasoning: Productive pass rusher entering his age-29 season with a new 3-year deal. The massive dead cap ($23.1M in year 1) makes a trade essentially impossible in 2026. Worth a 3rd-round pick on talent alone but the contract structure locks him in.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 3-year, $36.5M contract literally 3 days ago. The entire $23.1M in guarantees would accelerate as dead cap if traded. Financially impossible to move in 2026. He's here for at least 2 years.

players.name: Carlton Davis III
players.performance_evidence: Career-high 74.5 PFF defensive grade in 2024 with Lions (21st among CBs). 7th-lowest completion percentage allowed in man coverage (45.2%) per Next Gen Stats. 56 tackles, 2 INTs, 11 passes defended in 13 games with Detroit. Super Bowl champion with Buccaneers.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid CB2 on a 2-year deal with a $20M cap hit in 2026. At 29, he's past his physical prime but still productive. The guaranteed $13M remaining complicates a trade. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd-round pick to a CB-needy team, especially given the cap relief ($9M savings).

players.availability_reasoning: Signed as the CB2 opposite Gonzalez. His salary guarantee through 2026 makes him a lock. However, in 2027 there are no salary guarantees, making that a potential out. For now, he's a core starter who'd only move for significant return.
players.injury_notes: Broken jaw ended his 2024 season early in Detroit.

players.name: Christian Gonzalez
players.performance_evidence: Second-team All-Pro in 2024, Pro Bowl selection in 2025. 69 tackles, 10 pass deflections in 14 games in 2025 (missed first 3 with hamstring). Crucial playoff INT in AFC Championship vs. Broncos. 53.6% completion rate and 79.9 passer rating when targeted in 2025. Projected for $120-140M extension.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old All-Pro CB on a rookie deal with 5th-year option available. This is one of the most valuable player profiles in the NFL — young, elite, and cost-controlled at a premium position. He's worth multiple 1st-round picks. The extension candidate status (projected $120-140M) means acquiring teams would need to pay massive money on top of draft capital.

players.availability_reasoning: Franchise cornerstone. Second-team All-Pro and Pro Bowl in back-to-back years at age 23-24. The team has publicly committed to extending him. Eliot Wolf said they're 'excited about having him be a part of our team for the long term.' He is absolutely untouchable.
players.injury_notes: Torn labrum ended rookie season after 4 games (2023). Hamstring injury caused him to miss first 3 games of 2025. Concussion in late 2024. Health has been a minor concern but hasn't derailed his career.
players.trade_buzz: Extension talks expected. ESPN's Mike Reiss flagged the Gonzalez contract situation as a key offseason issue. Projected to command $120-140M based on Trent McDuffie's $124M deal with the Rams.

players.name: Marcus Jones
players.performance_evidence: Second-team All-Pro as a punt returner in 2025. AFC Defensive Player of the Month for October 2025. Led NFL in punt return average (21.6 yards). On defense: 2 INTs, 9 pass deflections, 1 sack in 2025 while playing 76% of defensive snaps as the nickel corner. Elite slot corner ranked top-5 in man coverage success rate. Named team captain.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite slot CB and All-Pro punt returner on a new team-friendly extension ($12M AAV, $6.3M cap hit in 2026). The dual-threat value — elite special teamer plus quality starter in the slot — is rare. Worth a mid-to-late 1st-round pick given the 3 years of control and below-market cap hit.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 3-year, $36M extension in October 2025. Team captain. Provides elite value as both a starter and punt returner. He's a cornerstone of both the defense and special teams. The Patriots locked him up specifically because they view him as irreplaceable.
players.injury_notes: Missed time in 2023 but has been healthy and durable in 2024-2025.
players.character_notes: Negotiated his own contract extension without an agent.

players.name: Brenden Schooler
players.performance_evidence: Primarily a special teams ace. Played limited defensive snaps in 2025 but was a core special teamer. Has been with the Patriots since going undrafted in 2022.

trade_value.reasoning: Special teams contributor with minimal defensive value. Essentially no trade value. Teams don't trade for special teamers.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth/special teams player. With Kevin Byard signing and Craig Woodson entrenched as a starter, Schooler's role is purely special teams. Moveable but nobody's calling.

players.name: Craig Woodson
players.performance_evidence: Started all 17 games as a rookie, playing 93.2% of defensive snaps (950 snaps — team leader). 79 tackles (4th on team), 4 TFLs. Dominated in playoffs: led NFL postseason with 30 tackles, 4 TFLs, 6 pass deflections, and an INT. PFF overall grade of 63.8 with a strong 78.5 run defense grade (20th among safeties). First Patriots rookie DB with 4 TFLs since stat was tracked.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old starting safety on a minimum rookie deal with 3 years of control. His playoff performance was outstanding. Coverage grade (56.1) needs work but the run defense and tackling are already NFL-caliber. Worth a 3rd-round pick based on age, control, and trajectory.

players.availability_reasoning: Emerged as a surprise starter and key piece of the defense. The team loves his work ethic and he's extremely cheap. With Kevin Byard signed as a veteran complement, Woodson's role is secure. Would only move for a significant overpay — he's a building block, not a trade chip.
players.injury_notes: Ankle injury caused him to miss a Wednesday practice but played through it.
